name: Cream of Broccoli Soup
recipeIngredient: 2 large broccoli stalks, flowerets removed and stems chopped
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large soup pot, heat the butter and olive oil over medium heat.
2. Add the onion, celery, garlic, and broccoli stems to the pot. Cook until the onion is translucent, about 5 minutes.
3. Stir in the flour, salt, black pepper, cayenne pepper, thyme, rosemary, and nutmeg. Cook for a few minutes more, stirring frequently.
4. Add the chicken stock, Madeira, and 4-5 broccoli flowerets to the pot. Mix well.
5. Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer, stirring occasionally until the vegetables are very soft, about 30 minutes.
6. While the soup is simmering, steam the remaining broccoli flowerets over boiling water for 7-10 minutes until bright green and crisp tender.
7. Remove the steamed flowerets from the steam and submerge in an ice water bath or cold running water until cool to touch. Drain and set aside.
8. Once the vegetables in the soup are very soft, remove it from heat and puree until very smooth.
9. Return the pot to heat and add the half and half. Stir well.
10. Simmer the soup for another 10 minutes.
11. Just before serving, add the steamed broccoli flowerets and a pinch more of each herb and Madeira, if desired. Serve in heated bowls.
